name: Standard Chartered Bank Zambia PLC
description: Standard Chartered Bank Zambia PLC is a subsidiary of Standard Chartered PLC, the London-listed emerging markets banking group. Listed on the LuSE Main Board since 1993, SCBL provides corporate banking, retail banking, and wealth management services in Zambia. The bank is a legacy institution in the Zambian financial sector with a focus on corporate and institutional clients. Standard Chartered Group regards Zambia as a key African market. The bank employs approximately 270 people.

financials.cashNote: Includes client deposits — not freely available cash
financials.debtNote: Bank borrowings — leverage is core to banking model
financials.marketDataNote: CRITICAL: StockAnalysis reports 2.08B shares outstanding at ZMW 1.28 (Jun 2026). A 1.25 forward split occurred Jan 7, 2026. Companies.json reports ZMW 7.20 (likely pre-split or different share count basis). Market cap ranges ZMW 2.67B (StockAnalysis) to ZMW 3.8B (companies.json). VERIFICATION NEEDED.

ratios.peNote: Based on StockAnalysis data; companies.json shows PE 7.5 — likely pre-split/stale
ratios.dividendNote: StockAnalysis shows no dividend; companies.json shows 7.2% yield. Standard Chartered historically paid dividends but may have suspended. CONFIRMATION NEEDED.

analystNotes.note: Standard Chartered Bank Zambia is a frustrating ticker to analyze: a January 2026 stock split and potential additional share issuance make historical comparisons difficult. TTM data shows ZMW 711M revenue, ZMW 191M profit, 26.8% net margin — solid banking metrics. But at PE 26.6x, it's the most expensive bank on LuSE vs ZNCO at 8.2x. Piotroski F-Score of 1 is concerning. The 10% free float means limited trading. Dividend status is unclear — StockAnalysis shows none, companies.json shows 7.2%. With an effective tax rate of 42%, earnings quality is poor. SCBL is a brand-name subsidiary, not an investable standalone bank.
analystNotes.author: Vituli Musukuma, PhD

riskRating: MEDIUM — OVERVALUED, LOW FLOAT, DATA INCONSISTENCIES
riskFactors: Extremely low free float (10%) — negligible secondary market
PE 26.6x — most expensive bank on LuSE
Piotroski F-Score of 1 — weak financial strength signal
Data inconsistencies between sources (price, dividend, share count)
42% effective tax rate — earnings quality concern
Small employee base (270) vs peers — limited retail banking scale
investmentThesis: Standard Chartered Bank Zambia is a subsidiary listing, not a genuine public company. With 10% free float and PE 26.6x, it offers neither value nor liquidity. For Zambian banking exposure, ZNCO (PE 8.2x, 5.8% yield, 35% float) is superior on every metric. SCBL is a 'heritage listing' — it trades on the Standard Chartered brand, not on investable fundamentals.
stance: AVOID — ZNCO IS THE SUPERIOR BANKING EXPOSURE
dataSources: Standard Chartered Bank Zambia FY2025 Annual Report (via LuSE SENS)
SCBL FY2024 Financial Statements (via MarketScreener, LuSE SENS)
dataQuality: LOW-MEDIUM — Major discrepancies between sources on price and dividend. Stock split on Jan 7, 2026 complicates historical comparison. Core P&L from audited reports confirmed. Market data reconciliation needed.
